summary: Subject<T>, BehaviorSubject<T>, ReplaySubject<T>, and AsyncSubject<T> behavior, trade-offs, and boundary-oriented usage guidance.
recommendedPatterns: Use Subject<T> only when bridging external callbacks or deliberate multicasting is required.
Choose the subject type based on replay and completion semantics, not convenience.
Audit every subject for ownership, terminal behavior, and subscriber expectations.
avoidPatterns: Do not use subjects as a default mutable state store.
Do not choose ReplaySubject<T> casually when memory retention matters.
setupSteps: Confirm whether a subject is necessary at all.
Pick Subject<T>, BehaviorSubject<T>, ReplaySubject<T>, or AsyncSubject<T> based on actual replay semantics.
Dispose subjects consistently with the owning component.
commonPitfalls: Unexpected replay or completion behavior for late subscribers.

sources.title: Subjects (Embedded Offline Catalog)
